content.claim: A 2004 centralization reform (Multi-Functional Micro-Regional Local Government Associations Act) halved the number of villages independently maintaining a primary school within four years, demonstrating that administrative and fiscal levers—not just demography—drive closure waves.
content.context: Demographic and administrative dynamics already in motion will force the closure or radical consolidation of primary schools in Hungarian villages with declining populations through 2040, unless explicit community-preservation or equity-based policy interventions are implemented to decouple school viability from enrollment-based cost thresholds.
limitations: The v1 source string was not re-verified during migration.
content.population: Population described in the migrated claim
